title: [2026] TRE 34
text:
IN THE JERSEY EMPLOYMENT AND DISCRIMINATION TRIBUNAL 

 BETWEEN 

 CHARLTON TAPLEY 

 CLAIMANT 

 AND 

 BLUE ISLANDS LIMITED (In
 Liquidation) 

 RESPONDENT 

 JUDGMENT IN DEFAULT 

 Reference: [2026]
TRE 34 

 Before:
 Advocate
Dr E Moran, Chairman 

 Decision 

 The Respondent must pay the Claimant damages in the sum of £15,838.94.

 Reasons 

 1. 
 The Claimant worked for the
Respondent as a pilot. As a condition of employment he was required by the
Respondent to pay a Type Rating Bond of over £20,000. The money would be repaid
to him monthly over a period of three years at the sum of £609.19 per month. He
received ten payments. 

 2. 
 His employment was
terminated on 17 November 2025 when the Respondent went into liquidation
leaving an outstanding debt of £15,838.94. 

 3. 
 The Respondent has not
repaid the money and has not filed a Response to the Claimant’s Claim Form. As
the claims are undefended, the Claimant is entitled to judgment in default
under Article 12 of the Employment and Discrimination Tribunal (Procedure)
(Jersey) Order 2016. 

 4. 
 I therefore order the
Respondent to pay the Claimant damages in the sum of £15,838.94. 

 Advocate Dr E Moran, Chairman Dated 3 February 2026 

 Page Last Updated: 11 Feb 2026
